task.name: Live Mode Configuration Modal
task.description: Replace the direct 'Start Live Simulation' button with a 'Configure Simulation...' button that opens a modal dialog exposing institutional parameters (bargaining and matching protocols) alongside simulation parameters, enabling users to answer the core research question: 'What difference does the institution make?'

vision_extraction.primary_goal: Enable users to configure institutional parameters before launching live simulations
success_criteria: Users can select bargaining protocol (Nash vs Rubinstein)
Users can select matching protocol (Opportunistic vs Stable Roommates)
Users can adjust simulation parameters without editing code
Quick-start remains possible by accepting defaults
deliverables: Configuration modal window with three sections

context.gap_analysis_summary: StartupSelector exposes only n_agents, grid_size, seed. Institutional parameters (bargaining_protocol, matching_protocol) and search parameters (perception_radius, discount_factor) are hardcoded in app.py. Need modal UI and parameter flow through run_visualization() to create_simple_economy().

phases.name: Configuration Modal Implementation
phases.description: Implement the full configuration modal with all sections, parameter flow, and integration
phases.exit_criteria: Modal opens, all parameters configurable, simulation launches with selected values

features.name: Extend LaunchConfig dataclass
features.description: Add fields for bargaining_protocol, matching_protocol, perception_radius, and discount_factor to LaunchConfig

features.name: Create LiveConfigModal class
features.description: New modal window class with three collapsible sections: Basic Parameters, Institutions, Search Parameters

functional: Modal window opens centered with appropriate size (~500x450)
Basic section: agents (int input), grid_size (int input), seed (int input)
Institutions section: bargaining protocol (radio), matching protocol (radio)
Cancel and Start Simulation buttons at bottom

features.name: Wire modal to StartupSelector
features.description: Rename button to 'Configure Simulation...', open modal on click, pass current defaults to modal

functional: Modal pre-fills with current StartupSelector values (n_agents, grid_size, seed)
Start returns LaunchConfig with all values

features.name: Extend run_visualization() signature
features.description: Add parameters for bargaining_protocol, matching_protocol, perception_radius, discount_factor

functional: Parameters have sensible defaults matching current behavior

features.name: Pass parameters through to create_simple_economy()
features.description: VisualizationApp passes all config params to create_simple_economy() on init and reset

functional: Reset (R key) recreates simulation with same parameters

features.name: Integration: run_with_startup_selector() flow
features.description: Complete flow from StartupSelector through modal to running simulation with selected parameters

functional: Full flow works: Startup -> Configure -> Modal -> Start -> Simulation runs
Selecting Rubinstein protocol results in simulation using RubinsteinBargainingProtocol
Selecting Stable Roommates results in simulation using StableRoommatesMatchingProtocol

resolved.choice: Separate modal window opened by 'Configure Simulation...' button
resolved.rationale: Keeps mode selection clean, provides focused configuration experience, supports quick-start by accepting defaults

resolved.choice: String identifiers ('nash', 'rubinstein', 'opportunistic', 'stable_roommates') rather than protocol instances
resolved.rationale: Dataclass remains simple/serializable; protocol instantiation happens at simulation creation
User preference for readability over technical precision in UI

escalation_triggers: Changes to simulation.py core logic beyond parameter passing
Breaking existing StartupSelector or ScenarioBrowser functionality
Changes to create_simple_economy() signature that break existing callers
